title: The Download: the state of AI, and protecting bears with drones
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.  If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whi

title: NASA is building the first nuclear reactor-powered interplanetary spacecraft. How will it work?
description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. Just before Artemis II began its historic slingshot around the moon, Jared Isaacman, the recently confirmed NASA administ

title: Coming soon: 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
description: Each year we compile our 10 Breakthrough Technologies list, featuring our educated predictions for which technologies will have the biggest impact on how we live and work. This year, however, we had a dilemma. While our final picks encompass all our core coverage areas (energy, AI, and biotech, plus

title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.

title: The problem with thinking you’re part Neanderthal
description: You’ve probably heard some version of this idea before: that many of us have an “inner Neanderthal.” That is to say, around 45,000 years ago, when Homo sapiens first arrived in Europe, they met members of a cousin species—the broad-browed, heavier-set Neanderthals—and, well, one thing led to another

title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
description: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.

title: You Can Soon Buy a $4,370 Humanoid Robot on AliExpress
description: Unitree is bringing its R1 to international markets. It arrives with some aerobatic capabilities and an entry-level price, but the question of what you'd actually do with it remains open.

title: Retro Rewind re-creates the glorious drudgery of working a '90s video store
description: What the nostalgic throwback lacks in complexity it makes up for in repetitive charm.

title: Measles takes a plane to Idaho, which has worst vaccination rate in US
description: In the 2024-2025 school year, only 78.5% of kindergartners had measles vaccination.

title: Google shoehorned Rust into Pixel 10 modem to make legacy code safer
description: Cellular modems are complex black boxes of legacy code, but Google is making them safer with Rust.

title: NZXT agrees to let customers keep their rental PCs in class-action settlement
description: NZXT will forgive up to $5,000 in debt for customers of the Flex program.

title: Your tech support company runs scams. Stop—or disguise with more fraud?
description: Fake it till you make it.

title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
description: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
description: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.

title: Meta Is Warned That Facial Recognition Glasses Will Arm Sexual Predators
description: More than 70 organizations, including the ACLU, EPIC, and Fight for the Future, say the AI smart glasses feature would endanger abuse victims, immigrants, and LGBTQ+ people.

title: Why opinion on AI is so divided
description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In an industry that doesn’t stand still, Stanford’s AI Index, an annual roundup of key results and trends, is a chance to take a breath. (It’s a marathon, not a s

title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
description: While many startups founded prior to the emergence of ChatGPT are struggling to position themselves for the AI era, Vercel, a 10-year-old dev tool and website hosting platform, is benefiting from the explosion of AI-generated apps and agents.

title: The Internet's Most Powerful Archiving Tool Is in Peril
description: As major news outlets cut off the Wayback Machine, journalists and advocacy groups are rallying to protect the Internet Archive’s vast collection of web pages.

title: AI Agents Are Coming for Your Dating Life
description: The developers of Pixel Societies are using AI agents to simulate social interactions. It's an attempt optimize the process of choosing new colleagues, friends, and even romantic partners.

title: The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business
description: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
